void_v0.doc: Address of the ZMOKE token contract this escrow operates on.\nSet at init; escrow refuses to talk to any other token.
void_v0.name: ZmokeMinter

tuple_v0.doc: intent_id \xe2\x86\x92 LockRecord. Persistent; TTL-bumped on access.
tuple_v0.name: Lock

function_v0.doc:
Transfer `amount` ZMOKE from `user` into this contract, held against
`intent_id`. User must have signed the transaction.

Uses `token.transfer()` (not `transfer_from` + allowance) so the user
only signs once. `require_auth(user)` here covers the outer call; the
user's tx-level auth tree covers the nested ZMOKE transfer since the
user is the token's `from`.
function_v0.name: lock

function_v0.doc: User cancels their own active lock; locked ZMOKE refunded to them.\nFails loudly if the caller isn't the original `lock.user` (auth check\nis against the address stored in the LockRecord, not the tx source).
function_v0.name: cancel

function_v0.doc:
Admin burns the locked ZMOKE on Stripe success. Status \xe2\x86\x92 Settled.

Calls `zmoke_minter.burn(self, amount)`. Self-burn is the standard
SEP-0041 path \xe2\x80\x94 no allowance dance needed, just contract-as-self auth
for the sub-invocation.
function_v0.name: settle

function_v0.doc:
Admin-gated in-place Wasm upgrade (CAP-0054). Preserves the contract
ID and every Lock(intent_id) persistent entry, plus the stored admin
and zmoke_minter addresses in instance storage.

Without this entrypoint, a Phase 4.5 re-deploy would mint a new
escrow contract ID and orphan every live LockRecord \xe2\x80\x94 meaning every
buyer mid-checkout loses their escrowed ZMOKE with no automated
recovery path. Catastrophic on mainnet.

Workflow: `stellar contract install` uploads the new Wasm blob and
returns its sha256 hash; this function swaps the running contract's
code to that hash. Caller must be the current admin.
function_v0.name: upgrade

function_v0.doc: Admin force-cancels a lock (post-expiry cleanup, dispute resolution).\nSame refund mechanics as user `cancel()`; distinct entrypoint so the\ncontract event log cleanly separates the two.
function_v0.name: admin_cancel

function_v0.doc:
CAP-0058 atomic constructor. Runs exactly once inside the deploy
transaction \xe2\x80\x94 closes the deploy-then-initialize front-run window
that would otherwise let an MEV bot claim admin between the two
calls. Addresses remain runtime parameters so the same WASM blob
works on testnet and mainnet.

Do NOT re-add an "already initialized" guard; the protocol
guarantees single-call semantics.

# Arguments
- `admin`        \xe2\x80\x94 treasury admin key (settles + force-cancels)
- `zmoke_minter` \xe2\x80\x94 address of the ZMOKE token contract
function_v0.name: __constructor
